matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: KCNJ11 neonatal-diabetes model (KCNJ11 KATP gain-of-function model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a KCNJ11 KATP channel and insulin-secretion review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology and Neonatal diabetes review context, Type 2 diabetes association research. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs5219 (KCNJ11 E23K) dosage suggests this sample carries the KCNJ11 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a KCNJ11 KATP channel and insulin-secretion review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology and Neonatal diabetes review context, Type 2 diabetes association research. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. This specific marker is curated as KCNJ11 diabetes association marker. rs5219 (KCNJ11 E23K) is curated as a KCNJ11 marker for Neonatal diabetes review context, Type 2 diabetes association research. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs5219 (KCNJ11 E23K) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the KCNJ11 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag ATP-sensitive potassium channel gating, beta-cell insulin secretion, neonatal diabetes biology, and sulfonylurea pharmacology.

cases.label: Gene variant found

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
